allusions.translator_note: The Greek epithet ptoliporthion (πτολίπορθιον, 'sacker of cities') is applied to Odysseus/Ulysses by Homer; Cicero quotes it in Greek script in the Latin text. Cicero uses the Homeric parallel to urge Plancus to be the finisher, like Ulysses rather than the brute fighters Ajax and Achilles.
